page: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dalv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz, configured with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 3078.57 and Geekbench single-core scoring 2022.0, both placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory-intensive metrics show limitations. The multi-core Geekbench score of 2600.0 and stress-ng multi-core performance of 3078.57 fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is constrained, with cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth at 151.31 GB/sec (bottom 10%) and Passmark database operations scoring 1038.92 (bottom 10%).

The D2als_v6 represents a low-memory, entry-level hardware profile. The dedicated AMD EPYC CPU provides reliable single-threaded execution, but the 4.0 GB RAM capacity and lack of local storage limit its suitability for memory-intensive or data-heavy applications. Qualitatively, it offers a cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated CPU cycles but have minimal memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, and simple text processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor clocked at 3.7 GHz with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads) and 4.0 GB of RAM. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth are weak, placing in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. This low-memory configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks with low memory requirements. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and simple web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a Dalv6 family server featuring a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a Dalv6 virtual machine with a 2-vCPU AMD EPYC CPU, 4.0 GB RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU AMD EPYC instance featuring strong single-core performance with a compact, low-memory footprint for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM with no local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
